_meta.purpose: Anatomical/conceptual references from BioRender public search API for nano-banana prompt enrichment. License-safe: stores only descriptions (public metadata), never assets.
_meta.usage: Read by scripts/generate-medical-icons.mjs when invoked with --enrich-with-bioicons. Description is concatenated to the Nano Banana prompt as conceptual guidance only — generated artwork must be ORIGINAL.

references.human-body-male-front: Minimal line drawing of an adult human body in anterior (front) view, showing a generic male figure with arms slightly extended outward and legs together in anatomical position.
references.human-body-female-front: A simple line drawing showing the full anterior view of an adult female human body in anatomical position, with arms slightly abducted from the sides and palms facing forward. The outline portrays the typical female body contour with proportional head, torso, and limbs.
references.spine: Posterior view of human vertebral column showing the characteristic S-curve with cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral and coccygeal regions, individual vertebrae visible.
references.lung-pair: Frontal view of human lungs showing the paired respiratory organs in pink with visible bronchial tree branching structure. The trachea extends upward, and the lungs are outlined by a thin border representing the pleural membrane.
references.heart-anatomical: Cross-sectional view of a human heart showing the internal four chambers (left/right atria and ventricles) and major vessels (aorta, pulmonary artery, vena cava). Three-quarter perspective, anatomical not symbolic.
references.kidney-pair: Pair of human kidneys with adrenal glands above each, posterior view, ureters extending downward toward the bladder. Renal vessels visible at the hilum.
references.liver: Simplified anatomical illustration of the human liver showing the classic two-lobed structure with the larger right lobe and smaller left lobe clearly visible. The largest internal organ, reddish-brown.
references.brain: Side profile of human brain depicted with the characteristic convoluted surface of the cerebral cortex showing sulci and gyri patterns. Cerebrum, cerebellum and brainstem distinguishable.
references.eye: Sagittal cross-section of human eye showing the eyeball with iris, pupil, sclera, retina, lens. Optic nerve extends posteriorly. Anatomical educational style.
references.ear: Cross-sectional view of human ear anatomy showing the external ear, middle ear with ossicles, and inner ear structures including the tympanic membrane, auditory canal, cochlea, and semicircular canals.
references.mask-n95: N95 respirator mask in three-quarter view with characteristic dome-shaped contoured design featuring curved top edge, white filtering surface, gray border around perimeter, and elastic ear loops. Designed for high-efficiency particulate filtration.
references.mask-surgical: Blue surgical mask shown from the front view, featuring horizontal pleats or folds across the mask surface and elastic ear loops on both sides. Pleated rectangular design.
references.gloves-medical: Pair of blue nitrile or latex examination gloves shown from the palm view, fully extended to display all five fingers, with slightly puffed cuffs. Ready for medical or laboratory use.
references.goggles-safety: Industrial safety goggles with full-coverage clear lenses, side ventilation slots, and elastic head strap. Side or three-quarter view.
references.helmet-safety: Yellow construction safety hard hat shown in side view, classic dome shape with brim and chin strap visible. Reflective accents not required. Industrial PPE.
references.hearing-protection: Pair of industrial earmuff hearing protectors connected by an adjustable headband, side or three-quarter view. Cup-shaped ear cushions.
references.fall-arrest-harness: Full-body fall arrest safety harness with shoulder straps, leg loops and dorsal D-ring connector at the back. Front view, no human figure inside, isolated.
references.pill: Single oblong pharmaceutical capsule depicted as a two-tone oval shape with a small circular highlight at the upper portion representing reflection/gloss. Solid colors, isolated.
references.syringe: Medical syringe with needle, plunger half-pressed, calibrated barrel with measurement markings, transparent body. Isolated horizontal orientation.
references.iv-bag: Transparent IV (intravenous) fluid bag with hanging hole at the top and delivery tube with connector at the bottom. Partially filled with clear or pale liquid solution.
references.first-aid-kit: Red first aid kit box featuring a white medical cross centered on the front. Closed, latched, three-quarter view with handle visible on top.
references.stethoscope: Medical stethoscope with simplified design showing a circular chest piece (diaphragm), connected to Y-shaped tubing that extends to form binaural earpieces at the top.
references.spirometer: Handheld digital spirometer device for measuring lung function, with a mouthpiece on top and a digital display visible on the front. Compact medical instrument.
references.audiometer: Audiometer headphones (large over-ear cups) with cable extending to a control box used for hearing tests. Clinical/diagnostic style.
references.thermometer: Modern digital infrared thermometer in gun-style ergonomic shape with display screen on top showing temperature reading. Trigger handle.
references.blood-pressure-cuff: Sphygmomanometer blood pressure cuff with manual bulb pump and circular dial gauge with measurement markings. Navy blue cuff connected to gauge via tubing.
references.pulse-oximeter: Fingertip pulse oximeter device with small digital display showing oxygen saturation reading. Compact clip shape with hinged design.
references.wheelchair: Manual wheelchair in three-quarter or side view showing the frame, large rear wheel with spokes, smaller front caster wheel, seat, and L-shaped backrest. No occupant.
references.crutch: Single underarm crutch with adjustable aluminum or wood frame, padded top support for the axilla, handgrip in the middle, and non-slip rubber tip at the bottom. Pair orientation acceptable.
references.arm-fracture: Forearm or arm in plaster cast or sling with supporting bandages, indicating the fracture site clearly. Clinical orthopedic illustration style.
references.leg-fracture: Lower leg with plaster cast on tibia/fibula, optional crutches nearby. Fracture location at lower leg suggested.
references.burn-skin: Cross-section diagram of skin showing the layers (epidermis, dermis, subcutaneous fat) with first/second/third degree burn depths annotated. Educational diagram style, NOT graphic.
references.cut-wound: Bandaged finger or wound site with gauze and adhesive tape, simple first-aid presentation. Clean and clinical, not graphic.
